props.text: 60% of AI Agent Incidents Are State Management. A 1989 Essay Said That Was Coming.

LangChain's State of Agent Engineering 2026 reports 60% of agent production incidents trace to state management — and the industry's answer is a $2.2B observability stack to wrap the state in more software.
Richard Gabriel's 1989 essay on Unix and C predicted exactly this: simplicity beats correctness, and the technically-worse architecture wins by spreading first.

The piece reads aeon's GitHub-Actions-cron architecture (PR #149's smithery-manifest schedule 0 6 1/7 * *, chain-runner.yml DEGRADED for six days, 95-tool MCP server now one-click from any Claude Desktop install) as the New Jersey side of that bet — worse on every LangSmith dashboard axis, and that's the point.

props.title: 60% of AI Agent Incidents Are State Management. A 1989 Essay Said That Was Coming.
